records.ety: [F. ombrage shade, suspicion, umbrage, L. umbraticus belonging to shade, fr. umbra a shade. Cf. Umber, Umbratic.]

defs: Shade; shadow; obscurity; hence, that which affords a shade, as a screen of trees or foliage.
quotes: Where highest woods, impenetrable To star or sunlight, spread their umbrage broad.
The opinion carries no show of truth nor umbrage of reason on its side.

defs: The feeling of being overshadowed; jealousy of another, as standing in one's light or way; hence, suspicion of injury or wrong; offense; resentment.
quotes: Which gave umbrage to wiser than myself.
Persons who feel most umbrage from the overshadowing aristocracy.
